between sessions when it matters most.

The officer assigned as the sniper is
Jared Tadahara, a long-erving police
officer who moved from patrol and street
crime work into a specialized SWAT
sniper role. After arriving on scene,
Tadhara conducts a quick tactical
assessment and moves into a nearby house
facing the Samson home. From here, he
has a clear view of the backside of the
house, a position that allows
observation without being easily visible
to the suspect and provides a safe
distance to avoid interfering with
perimeter officers. But Tadihara isn't
working alone. Every sniper has a
spotter, a second officer responsible
for range calculations, wind
assessments, and most critically,
confirming that the shot is clear before
Tadihara pulls the trigger. Across the
street, negotiations continue. For the
